name: Google Custom Search MCP Server
what_it_does: Google Custom Search Engine (CSE) MCP server enabling AI agents to perform Google searches — querying the Google Custom Search API for web results, retrieving structured search result data with titles, URLs, and snippets, and integrating Google's search index into agent-driven research and information retrieval workflows.
use_cases: Searching the web via Google from research agents
Retrieving structured search results with metadata from data agents
Searching specific websites or domains via Custom Search Engine config
Validating URLs and finding current content from fact-checking agents
Building search-augmented workflows from RAG agents
Finding documentation and references from coding assistant agents
not_for: AI-synthesized answers (raw search results only; use Perplexity for synthesis)
Image search without CSE configuration for image results
Teams preferring Brave Search or Serper for simpler pricing
best_when: An agent needs raw Google Search results with structured metadata — titles, URLs, snippets — via the official Google API rather than scraping.
avoid_when: You need AI-synthesized answers or have high search volume — Serper may be cheaper for bulk search queries.

auth.notes: Google API key and Custom Search Engine ID (cx parameter) required. Both obtained from Google Cloud Console. Free tier: 100 queries/day; paid: $5/1,000 queries.

pricing.notes: 100 free queries/day without credit card. Beyond that, $5/1,000 queries billed via Google Cloud. Must set up Custom Search Engine at programmablesearchengine.google.com.

performance.rate_limits: 100 queries/day free; 10,000 queries/day paid max per project

known_agent_gotchas: Requires both an API key AND a Custom Search Engine ID (cx) — two values to configure
Free tier is only 100 queries/day — easy to exhaust in automated agent workflows
Custom Search Engine must be configured before use — can be set to search entire web
Results max at 10 per page, 100 results total per query
Community MCP — not official Google MCP; verify it uses CSE API correctly
Google API key should be restricted to Custom Search API only in Cloud Console
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key authentication. GDPR compliant. Community MCP. Restrict API key to Custom Search API in Google Cloud Console. Store key as environment variable.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key restricted to CSE API. GDPR. Community MCP. Store key as env var.
